mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.09% signals an accommodative monetary stance. Sustained low rates compress long-term discount rates and reduce term premium, causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into duration.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 10 basis points over 20 trading days in at least 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 6.3% signals labor market slack. If UNRATE rises above 6.5% within the next 60 trading days, it triggers recession expectations, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as investors seek safe-haven duration and the Fed cuts rates.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 6.5% within 60 trading days
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with deteriorating labor conditions
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises when UNRATE crosses above 6.5%, or forward 60-day returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 434,571M and housing starts (HOUST) at 946K indicate solid demand. A joint decline in both RSAFS and HOUST by more than 5% month-over-month signals demand destruction, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 5% MoM AND HOUST declines > 5% MoM simultaneously
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points when both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 5% MoM, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 102.74 (2017=100) reflects supply-side capacity. A sustained decline in INDPRO below 102.0 over 3 months signals supply constraints and stagflation risk, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as inflation expectations increase and real rates compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 102.0 and remains there for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 20 basis points when INDPRO declines below 102.0 for 3 months, or forward 60-day returns are negative in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 15,946.6B and payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 138,252K indicate economic expansion. Accelerating PAYEMS growth above 200K per month combined with GDPC1 growth above 2.5% annualized signals strong demand, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as growth expectations increase and the Fed maintains higher rates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly gain > 200K AND GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5%
regime_hint: strong expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 15 basis points when both PAYEMS > 200K and GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 236.254 (1982-1984=100) reflects current inflation. If CPI rises more than 0.4% month-over-month, it signals inflation acceleration that forces the Fed to maintain or raise rates, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as inflation expectations and term premium increase.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 10 basis points within 5 trading days when CPI rises > 0.4% MoM, or forward 5-day returns are positive in > 65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 138,252K and unemployment at 6.3% show labor market resilience. A joint signal of PAYEMS declining below 50K monthly gains AND UNRATE rising above 6.8% signals labor market deterioration, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as recession risk increases and safe-haven demand rises.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly gain < 50K AND UNRATE rises above 6.8%
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 20 basis points when PAYEMS < 50K and UNRATE > 6.8%, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 946K indicate residential demand. A sustained decline in HOUST below 900K for 2 consecutive months signals housing market weakness and forward economic slowdown, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as growth expectations compress and the Fed signals rate cuts.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 900K for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 25 basis points when HOUST remains below 900K for 2 months, or forward 60-day returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.
